title: triticum polonicum var. turgidum
intro: Triticum polonicum var. turgidum, also known as Polish wheat or Polish rye, is a type of wild grass that is native to Eastern Europe. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Triticum polonicum var. turgidum include reducing inflammation, improving gut health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
